title: Smoky Roasted Ukrainian Zucchini Roast
description: A kid-friendly, lowfat Ukrainian dish that combines smoky roasted zucchini with a tangy fermented yogurt sauce and a crunchy sauerkraut topping.

ingredients.name: zucchini
ingredients.amount: 4 medium (≈600 g), sliced lengthwise into 1 cm strips

ingredients.name: fermented beet juice (or apple cider vinegar)

instructions: Season the zucchini strips with smoked paprika, minced garlic, sliced onion, salt & pepper, and drizzle with olive oil. Toss until evenly coated.
Arrange the seasoned zucchini on a parchment‑lined baking sheet in a single layer.
Roast for 20–25 minutes, turning once, until the edges are golden and the zucchini is tender.
While the zucchini roasts, whisk together the strained Greek yogurt, tomato paste, fermented beet juice, dried dill, and lemon zest until smooth.
When the zucchini is done, transfer it to a serving dish and top with the drained sauerkraut.
Drizzle the yogurt sauce over the zucchini and sauerkraut, then sprinkle with fresh parsley if desired.
Let the roast rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve warm as a hearty side or a light main course.
Enjoy the smoky, fermented twist with a side of fresh bread or a simple salad.
